name: ddpm

members.name: DDPMConfig
members.signature: DDPMConfig(sample_size: int | tuple[int, int] = 32, in_channels: int = 3, out_channels: int = 3, act_fn: GenerativeActivation = 'silu', num_train_timesteps: int = 1000, beta_start: float = 0.0001, beta_end: float = 0.02, beta_schedule: BetaSchedule = 'linear', prediction_type: Literal['epsilon', 'sample', 'v_prediction'] = 'epsilon', base_channels: int = 128, channel_mult: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (1, 2, 2, 2))(), num_res_blocks: int = 2, attention_resolutions: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (16,))(), num_heads: int = 1, dropout: float = 0.1, resnet_groups: int = 32, learn_sigma: bool = False, clip_denoised: bool = True)

methods.name: out_channels_effective
methods.summary:
How many channels the U-Net actually emits (doubled when
`learn_sigma=True`).

methods.name: __init__
methods.signature: __init__(sample_size: int | tuple[int, int] = 32, in_channels: int = 3, out_channels: int = 3, act_fn: GenerativeActivation = 'silu', num_train_timesteps: int = 1000, beta_start: float = 0.0001, beta_end: float = 0.02, beta_schedule: BetaSchedule = 'linear', prediction_type: Literal['epsilon', 'sample', 'v_prediction'] = 'epsilon', base_channels: int = 128, channel_mult: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (1, 2, 2, 2))(), num_res_blocks: int = 2, attention_resolutions: tuple[int, ...] = (lambda: (16,))(), num_heads: int = 1, dropout: float = 0.1, resnet_groups: int = 32, learn_sigma: bool = False, clip_denoised: bool = True)

parameters.name: base_channels
parameters.description:
Channel width of the first U-Net stage `C_0`.  All
other stages are integer multiples (see `channel_mult`).
Paper default: 128.

parameters.name: channel_mult
parameters.description:
Per-stage channel multiplier.  `(1, 2, 2, 2)` for
CIFAR (Table 9), `(1, 1, 2, 2, 4, 4)` for LSUN/CelebA-HQ.
Length = number of downsampling stages; spatial resolution
divides by `2 ** (L - 1)` (no downsample after the final
stage).

parameters.name: num_res_blocks
parameters.description: ResBlocks per stage (paper: 2).

parameters.name: attention_resolutions
parameters.description:
Spatial sizes at which self-attention is
applied.  Ho 2020 uses `(16,)` — attention only at the
16×16 feature scale.

parameters.name: num_heads
parameters.description: Attention head count (paper: 1 for CIFAR, can scale).

parameters.name: dropout
parameters.description:
Dropout inside ResBlocks.  Paper: 0.1 (CIFAR), 0.0
(CelebA / LSUN).

parameters.name: resnet_groups
parameters.description:
`GroupNorm` group count inside ResBlocks (paper
default: 32).  Must divide every per-stage channel count.

parameters.name: learn_sigma
parameters.description:
Improved-DDPM (Nichol 2021) — when `True`, the
network predicts the variance in addition to the mean.  Output
channels become `2 * in_channels`.

parameters.name: clip_denoised
parameters.description:
Clip the predicted `x_0` to `[-1, 1]` during
sampling (default True).

members.name: DDPMForImageGeneration

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: forward(sample: Tensor, timestep: Tensor, target: Tensor | None = None)
methods.summary: Predict noise (or `[noise, σ²]`) at the given timestep.

parameters.name: sample
parameters.description: `(B, in_channels, H, W)` noisy image `x_t`.

parameters.name: timestep
parameters.description:
`(B,)` or `()` int timestep tensor.  Scalar tensors
are broadcast.

returns.description: `(B, out_channels_effective, H, W)` raw network output.

members.summary: DDPM with the noise-prediction training loss and Markov-chain sampler.
members.extended:
Wraps a `DDPMUNet` denoiser, supplies the Ho 2020 §3.2
"simplified" training objective when `target` is provided, and inherits
`DiffusionMixin.generate` for ancestral sampling over the configured
noise schedule.  This is the standard entry point for training and
inference on the DDPM family.

Training
    * `forward(sample, timestep)` returns the raw network output.
    * `forward(sample, timestep, target=...)` (`target` = ground-truth
      noise for `epsilon` parameterisation) additionally fills the
      `loss` field with the MSE loss of the paper's simple objective
      (Ho 2020 Eq. 14).

Sampling
    * Use `generate` from `DiffusionMixin` — pass any
      `Scheduler` (`DDPMScheduler` for ancestral sampling).

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters.  `config.learn_sigma=True` is rejected at
construction time — Improved-DDPM's hybrid
$L_{\text{simple}} + \lambda L_{\text{vlb}}$ loss is not yet
implemented in Lucid.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import (
...     DDPMConfig, DDPMForImageGeneration,
... )
>>> cfg = DDPMConfig(sample_size=32, base_channels=32,
...                  channel_mult=(1, 2), num_res_blocks=1,
...                  resnet_groups=16, num_train_timesteps=100)
>>> model = DDPMForImageGeneration(cfg).eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([42]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 32, 32)
(1, 3, 32, 32)
notes:
Reference: Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, *"Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic
Models"*, NeurIPS, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.11239](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239)); Improved-DDPM additions
from Nichol and Dhariwal, ICML 2021 ([arXiv:2102.09672](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.09672)).

Training loss (when `target` is supplied):



$$
\mathcal{L}_{\text{simple}}(\theta)
    = \mathbb{E}_{t, x_0, \epsilon}\!\left[
        \big\lVert \epsilon - \epsilon_\theta(x_t, t)
        \big\rVert^2
      \right],
$$

where $x_t = \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}_t} x_0 + \sqrt{1 - \bar{\alpha}_t}\, \epsilon$.

members.name: DDPMModel

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Predict noise (or `[noise, σ²]`) at the given timestep.

parameters.name: sample
parameters.description: `(B, in_channels, H, W)` noisy image `x_t`.

parameters.name: timestep
parameters.description:
`(B,)` or `()` int timestep tensor.  Scalar tensors
are broadcast.

returns.description: `(B, out_channels_effective, H, W)` raw network output.

members.summary: Bare DDPM U-Net trunk with the sampling-capable `DiffusionMixin`.
members.extended:
Returns a `DiffusionModelOutput` whose `sample` field is the
raw network output — interpreted as predicted noise $\epsilon$,
clean signal $x_0$, or velocity $v$ depending on
`config.prediction_type`.  When `config.learn_sigma` is `True`
the output channels are `2 * in_channels`: the first half is the
mean prediction, the second half the (raw) variance prediction;
downstream code splits the two if needed.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters governing the U-Net topology and the diffusion
noise schedule (`num_train_timesteps`, `beta_start`,
`beta_end`, `beta_schedule`, `prediction_type`).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import DDPMConfig, DDPMModel
>>> cfg = DDPMConfig(sample_size=32, base_channels=32,
...                  channel_mult=(1, 2), num_res_blocks=1,
...                  resnet_groups=16, num_train_timesteps=100)
>>> model = DDPMModel(cfg).eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([42]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 32, 32) — predicted noise
(1, 3, 32, 32)
notes:
Reference: Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, *"Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic
Models"*, NeurIPS, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.11239](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239)).

The forward Markov chain has the closed-form marginal



$$
q(x_t \mid x_0)
    = \mathcal{N}\!\big(
        x_t;\;
        \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}_t}\, x_0,\;
        (1 - \bar{\alpha}_t)\, \mathbf{I}
      \big),
$$

with $\bar{\alpha}_t = \prod_{s=1}^{t} (1 - \beta_s)$.  The
U-Net is trained to predict the injected noise so reverse sampling
iterates $t = T, T-1, \ldots, 1$ via the standard ancestral
Gaussian update.

attributes.name: base_model_prefix
attributes.description:
State-dict prefix (`"ddpm"`) under which the trunk is nested in
task-head variants.

members.name: DDPMUNet

methods.name: forward
methods.summary: Predict noise (or `[noise, σ²]`) at the given timestep.

parameters.name: sample
parameters.description: `(B, in_channels, H, W)` noisy image `x_t`.

parameters.name: timestep
parameters.description:
`(B,)` or `()` int timestep tensor.  Scalar tensors
are broadcast.

returns.description: `(B, out_channels_effective, H, W)` raw network output.

members.summary: U-Net denoiser shared by every DDPM variant.
members.extended:
Implements the architecture introduced by Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, 2020:
a symmetric U-Net augmented with sinusoidal-timestep conditioning,
GroupNorm-equipped residual blocks, and self-attention at low-resolution
feature maps.  The encoder reduces spatial resolution by a factor of
$2^{L-1}$ over $L = \mathrm{len}(\text{channel\_mult})$
stages; the middle block applies ResBlock → Attn → ResBlock at the
bottleneck; the decoder mirrors the encoder with concatenated skip
connections.  The output convolution emits `out_channels_effective`
channels — equal to `in_channels` (or `2 * in_channels` when
`learn_sigma` is enabled for variance prediction).

Spatial layout per stage $l \in [0, L)$:
    * `num_res_blocks` ResBlocks (with optional attention at
      `attention_resolutions`).
    * Downsample → next stage (skipped for `l == L - 1`).

Decoder mirrors the encoder with concatenated skip features; the extra
ResBlock per stage (`num_res_blocks + 1` total on the decoder side)
consumes the post-downsample skip.

parameters.name: config
parameters.description:
Hyperparameters controlling `base_channels`, `channel_mult`,
`num_res_blocks`, `attention_resolutions`, `num_heads`,
`dropout`, `resnet_groups`, and `learn_sigma`.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import DDPMConfig
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm._model import DDPMUNet
>>> cfg = DDPMConfig(sample_size=32, base_channels=32,
...                  channel_mult=(1, 2), num_res_blocks=1,
...                  attention_resolutions=(16,), resnet_groups=16)
>>> unet = DDPMUNet(cfg).eval()
>>> x = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([42]).long()
>>> out = unet(x, t)
>>> out.shape   # (1, 3, 32, 32)
(1, 3, 32, 32)
notes:
Reference: Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, *"Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic
Models"*, NeurIPS, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.11239](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239)); architecture details in
Appendix B.  Improved-DDPM additions follow Nichol and Dhariwal, *
"Improved Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models"*, ICML, 2021
([arXiv:2102.09672](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.09672)).

ResBlock conditioning recipe:



$$
h = \mathrm{Conv}_2\!\big(
    \mathrm{SiLU}(
        \mathrm{Norm}_2(
            \mathrm{SiLU}(t \cdot W_t) + \mathrm{Conv}_1(
                \mathrm{SiLU}(\mathrm{Norm}_1(x))
            )
        )
    )
\big)
+ \mathrm{Skip}(x).
$$

attributes.name: time_mlp
attributes.description:
Sinusoidal timestep embedding + 2-layer MLP producing the per-
timestep conditioning vector of width `4 * base_channels`.

attributes.name: conv_in
attributes.description: Stem convolution lifting input channels to `base_channels`.

attributes.name: down_res, down_attn, down_sample
attributes.description: Encoder ResBlocks, optional AttentionBlocks, and Downsamples.

attributes.name: mid_block1, mid_attn, mid_block2
attributes.description: Bottleneck stack at the lowest spatial resolution.

attributes.name: up_res, up_attn, up_sample
attributes.description: Decoder ResBlocks, optional AttentionBlocks, and Upsamples.

attributes.name: norm_out
attributes.description: Output GroupNorm before the final convolution.

members.name: ddpm_cifar
members.signature: ddpm_cifar(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: DDPMCifarWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Construct a DDPM U-Net trunk for the CIFAR-10 setup.
members.extended:
Paper-faithful CIFAR-10 configuration from Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, 2020
Appendix B.1 / Table 9: sample size 32x32, `base_channels=128`,
`channel_mult=(1, 2, 2, 2)` (four spatial stages — 32 -> 16 -> 8 ->
4), 2 ResBlocks per stage, self-attention at the 16x16 feature map,
1 attention head, dropout 0.1, and a 1000-step linear `beta`
schedule.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` → the official
`google/ddpm-cifar10-32` checkpoint
(`DDPMCifarWeights.CIFAR10`) — the trained noise predictor
$\epsilon_\theta(x_t, t)$.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `DDPMConfig` field overrides (e.g. `dropout=...`,
`num_train_timesteps=...`) forwarded into the underlying config.
Overrides that change the U-Net shape are incompatible with the
pretrained checkpoint.

returns.description:
Bare U-Net trunk configured with the CIFAR-10 setup (pretrained
when requested).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import ddpm_cifar
>>> model = ddpm_cifar().eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([42]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 32, 32) — predicted noise
(1, 3, 32, 32)
notes:
Reference: Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, *"Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic
Models"*, NeurIPS, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.11239](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239)), Appendix B.1.

members.name: ddpm_cifar_gen
members.signature: ddpm_cifar_gen(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: DDPMCifarWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Construct a DDPM CIFAR-10 model with training loss and `.generate()`.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `ddpm_cifar` (sample size 32x32, 4-stage U-Net),
wrapped with the Ho 2020 simplified training objective and
`DiffusionMixin.generate` for ancestral sampling.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` → the official
`google/ddpm-cifar10-32` checkpoint
(`DDPMCifarWeights.CIFAR10`), giving an inference-ready
sampler — `model.generate(...)` draws CIFAR-10-like images.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `DDPMConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

returns.description:
CIFAR-10 DDPM wrapped with the noise-prediction loss head
(pretrained when requested).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import ddpm_cifar_gen
>>> model = ddpm_cifar_gen().eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 32, 32))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([42]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 32, 32)
(1, 3, 32, 32)
notes:
Reference: Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, *"Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic
Models"*, NeurIPS, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.11239](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239)), Appendix B.1.

members.name: ddpm_imagenet64
members.summary: Construct an Improved-DDPM U-Net trunk for ImageNet 64x64.
members.extended:
Paper-faithful ImageNet 64x64 configuration from Nichol and Dhariwal,
2021 Table 1: sample size 64x64, `base_channels=128`,
`channel_mult=(1, 2, 3, 4)` (four spatial stages — 64 -> 32 -> 16
-> 8), 3 ResBlocks per stage, multi-scale self-attention at the 8 /
16 / 32 feature maps, 4 attention heads, learned variance head
enabled (`learn_sigma=True`), and a 4000-step cosine `beta`
schedule.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `DDPMConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

returns.description:
Bare U-Net trunk configured with the Improved-DDPM ImageNet 64x64
setup and any overrides.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import ddpm_imagenet64
>>> model = ddpm_imagenet64().eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 64, 64))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([1234]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # learn_sigma=True -> 2 * 3 = 6 channels
(1, 6, 64, 64)
notes:
Reference: Nichol and Dhariwal, *"Improved Denoising Diffusion
Probabilistic Models"*, ICML, 2021 ([arXiv:2102.09672](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.09672)), Table 1.

members.name: ddpm_imagenet64_gen
members.summary: Construct an Improved-DDPM ImageNet 64x64 model with training loss and `.generate()`.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `ddpm_imagenet64` (sample size 64x64, 4-stage U-Net
with `learn_sigma=True`).

> **Warning:**
>
> This factory currently raises `NotImplementedError` at
> construction time — the default config has `learn_sigma=True`,
> which requires the Improved-DDPM hybrid
> $L_{\text{simple}} + \lambda L_{\text{vlb}}$ loss not yet
> implemented in Lucid.  Pass `learn_sigma=False` to override.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description: Reserved for future weight registration; currently a no-op.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `DDPMConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import ddpm_imagenet64_gen
>>> model = ddpm_imagenet64_gen(learn_sigma=False).eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 64, 64))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([1234]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 64, 64)
(1, 3, 64, 64)
notes:
Reference: Nichol and Dhariwal, *"Improved Denoising Diffusion
Probabilistic Models"*, ICML, 2021 ([arXiv:2102.09672](https://arxiv.org/abs/2102.09672)), Table 1.

members.name: ddpm_lsun
members.signature: ddpm_lsun(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: DDPMChurchWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Construct a DDPM U-Net trunk for the LSUN / CelebA-HQ 256x256 setup.
members.extended:
Paper-faithful 256x256 configuration from Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, 2020
Appendix B.2 (LSUN church / bedroom and CelebA-HQ): sample size
256x256, `base_channels=128`, `channel_mult=(1, 1, 2, 2, 4, 4)`
(six spatial stages — 256 -> 128 -> ... -> 8), 2 ResBlocks per stage,
self-attention at the 16x16 feature map, 1 attention head, no
dropout, and a 1000-step linear `beta` schedule.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` → the official
`google/ddpm-church-256` LSUN-Church checkpoint
(`DDPMChurchWeights.LSUN_CHURCH`).

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `DDPMConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.  Overrides that change the U-Net shape are
incompatible with the pretrained checkpoint.

returns.description:
Bare U-Net trunk configured with the LSUN / CelebA-HQ setup
(pretrained when requested).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import ddpm_lsun
>>> model = ddpm_lsun().eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 256, 256))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([500]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 256, 256)
(1, 3, 256, 256)
notes:
Reference: Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, *"Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic
Models"*, NeurIPS, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.11239](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239)), Appendix B.2.

members.name: ddpm_lsun_gen
members.signature: ddpm_lsun_gen(pretrained: bool | str = False, weights: DDPMChurchWeights | None = None, overrides: object = {})
members.summary: Construct a DDPM LSUN / CelebA-HQ model with training loss and `.generate()`.
members.extended:
Same trunk as `ddpm_lsun` (sample size 256x256, 6-stage U-Net),
wrapped with the Ho 2020 simplified training objective and
`DiffusionMixin.generate` for ancestral sampling.

parameters.name: pretrained
parameters.description:
Weight selector.  `False` → random init; `True` → the official
`google/ddpm-church-256` checkpoint
(`DDPMChurchWeights.LSUN_CHURCH`), giving an inference-ready
sampler — `model.generate(...)` draws LSUN-Church-like images.

parameters.name: weights
parameters.description: Explicit weights enum member; takes precedence over `pretrained`.

parameters.name: **overrides
parameters.description:
Optional `DDPMConfig` field overrides forwarded into the
underlying config.

returns.description:
LSUN / CelebA-HQ DDPM wrapped with the noise-prediction loss head
(pretrained when requested).

examples:
>>> import lucid
>>> from lucid.models.generative.ddpm import ddpm_lsun_gen
>>> model = ddpm_lsun_gen().eval()
>>> x_t = lucid.randn((1, 3, 256, 256))
>>> t = lucid.tensor([500]).long()
>>> out = model(x_t, t)
>>> out.sample.shape   # (1, 3, 256, 256)
(1, 3, 256, 256)
notes:
Reference: Ho, Jain, and Abbeel, *"Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic
Models"*, NeurIPS, 2020 ([arXiv:2006.11239](https://arxiv.org/abs/2006.11239)), Appendix B.2.

summary: DDPM family — Ho et al., 2020 ("Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models").
citation: Ho, Jonathan, et al. "Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models." Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems, 2020, pp. 6840–6851.
theory:
DDPM — *Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models* — defines a latent
variable generative model through a pair of Markov chains.  The
**forward (noising) chain** progressively corrupts data
$x_0 \sim q(x_0)$ over $T$ steps with a fixed Gaussian
schedule $\{\beta_t\}_{t=1}^{T}$:



$$
q(x_t \mid x_{t-1})
    = \mathcal{N}\!\left(
        x_t;\; \sqrt{1 - \beta_t}\, x_{t-1},\; \beta_t\, \mathbf{I}
      \right).
$$

With $\alpha_t = 1 - \beta_t$ and
$\bar{\alpha}_t = \prod_{s=1}^{t} \alpha_s$, the marginal admits a
closed form that supports cheap one-shot sampling at any step:



$$
q(x_t \mid x_0)
    = \mathcal{N}\!\left(
        x_t;\; \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}_t}\, x_0,\;
        (1 - \bar{\alpha}_t)\, \mathbf{I}
      \right),
\qquad
x_t = \sqrt{\bar{\alpha}_t}\, x_0 +
      \sqrt{1 - \bar{\alpha}_t}\, \epsilon,\;
      \epsilon \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \mathbf{I}).
$$

The **reverse (denoising) chain** is parameterised by a U-Net
$\epsilon_\theta(x_t, t)$ trained to predict the injected noise.
Ho et al. show that the variational ELBO



$$
\mathcal{L}_{\text{ELBO}} = \mathbb{E}_q\!\left[
    \log \frac{q(x_{1:T} \mid x_0)}{p_\theta(x_{0:T})}
\right]
$$

decomposes into per-timestep KL divergences which, after a
reweighting, simplify to the deceptively elegant noise-prediction
objective



$$
\mathcal{L}_{\text{simple}}(\theta) =
    \mathbb{E}_{t, x_0, \epsilon}\!\left[
        \big\lVert \epsilon -
            \epsilon_\theta(x_t, t)
        \big\rVert^2
    \right].
$$

Sampling iterates $t = T, T-1, \dots, 1$ via
$x_{t-1} = \tfrac{1}{\sqrt{\alpha_t}} \big(x_t - \tfrac{\beta_t}{\sqrt{1 - \bar{\alpha}_t}}\, \epsilon_\theta(x_t, t)\big) + \sigma_t z$.  The U-Net itself follows
the original architecture (Ronneberger 2015) augmented with
sinusoidal timestep embeddings, self-attention at low-resolution
feature maps (default $16 \times 16$), and GroupNorm-equipped
residual blocks — the now-canonical recipe inherited by ADM, Imagen,
Stable Diffusion, and beyond.
